title: poa pratensis subsp. stenachyra
intro: Poa pratensis subsp. stenachyra, commonly known as the Kentucky bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Poa pratensis subsp. stenachyra can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
